cards.title: Having children makes you smarter? Incredible, but true
cards.body: Recent studies show mothers and fathers have healthier, younger-looking brains than non-parents. The mental and emotional gymnastics of parenting may actually boost cognitive function.

cards.title: Sometimes Your Child Isn't Triggering You: Your Childhood Is
cards.body: Strong parenting reactions often stem from unresolved childhood experiences rather than your child's behavior. Understanding this connection helps parents respond more thoughtfully to their children.

cards.title: When a Child's Disability or Mental Illness Hurts Siblings
cards.body: Children with disabilities or mental illnesses may behave aggressively toward siblings, creating safety concerns. Parents can take steps to help all their children and maintain household safety.

cards.title: When Picky Eating Is Actually Anxiety
cards.body: Children refusing food may actually be avoiding fear rather than being stubborn eaters. Recognizing anxiety as the root cause helps parents address the real issue.

cards.title: We Are Experiencing an Educational Crisis
cards.body: U.S. test scores have been declining for a generation, signaling a serious educational problem. Parents should be aware and advocate for better learning opportunities for their children.

cards.title: Preparing for College: 6 Mental Health Tips for Summer
cards.body: Families can take practical steps before college starts to support their student's mental wellness. From paperwork to mindset work, summer preparation sets up success.

cards.title: Online marketplaces still selling dozens of unsafe baby products
cards.body: Amazon, TikTok and other sites continue selling pillows, sleeping bags and feeders that have safety notices. Parents should be cautious when purchasing baby items from online marketplaces.

cards.title: Arizona toddler found alive in hospital morgue after declared dead
cards.body: An 18-month-old who was declared dead from drowning was discovered alive in the hospital morgue. The child was quickly transferred and survived the incident.

cards.title: The one change that worked: I banned myself from social media
cards.body: A psychotherapist discovered that limiting her phone usage improved her mood and family relationships dramatically. Setting strict boundaries on social media helps parents become calmer and more present.
